metadata.title:
A FINISH
FIGHT WITH


v, v. P

pages.raw_text:
The hopes Of some OF the more
optimistic members of the local
electrical workers union that the
Washington Water Power company
would come to terms with their
striking employees seems to have
gone glimmering. Among those
who have known the policy and the
viniictiveness Of the local monop-
oily there has never been a doubt
but that they would continue the
fight for low wages and long hours.
The strikers are in a position to
continue the fight Indefinitely on
account OF the fact that they are
a., employed by other companies.


II seems strange to union men


tina. the Traction company and all
the contractors in the city can af-
ford ~ pay the electrical workers
decent wages while the Washing
ton Water Power company refuses
- Go the same. The officers of
The company are outspoken in
favor 81 cheap wages and cheap
labor. Local union men say that
the monopoly should confine its
business enterprises to the south
e-n states, where negro labor can
De secured.
